strategic_profile.executive_summary: UC San Diego is a top-tier public research university known for its rigorous STEM, biological science, and oceanography programs. It features a unique eight-college system that provides smaller residential communities within a large R1 research institution.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The acceptance rate has stabilized around 26-28%, with in-state admission becoming slightly more restrictive (24.6% in 2025).
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants must present exceptionally high GPAs (middle 50% 4.11-4.28) and strong essays to be competitive.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The university employs a highly restrictive 'Selective Major' points system for internal transfers into high-demand fields like Computer Science.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Prospective STEM students must apply directly to their preferred major and select a non-capped alternate major.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The 'UC Socially Dead' moniker persists due to the sprawling, decentralized campus layout and rigorous academic focus.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students must be proactive in seeking out social opportunities through clubs, co-ops, and Greek life to build a community.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: A relatively quiet campus where social life is decentralized. Much of the interaction is driven by student organizations, co-ops like Groundwork Books, and events hosted by the individual residential colleges.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: The 2,000+ acre campus is expansive, making walking between classes time-consuming. Students heavily rely on campus shuttles, bicycles, and the UC San Diego Central Campus trolley station.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: As a billion-dollar research enterprise, UCSD offers massive opportunities for undergraduates to participate in cutting-edge lab work, particularly in health, engineering, and environmental sciences.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-blind/Test-free. SAT/ACT scores are not used in admissions decisions but may be used for course placement after enrollment.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist ranked according to CDS, though admissions office states it is unranked.

testing.policy_note: UC San Diego is test-blind. Standardized test scores are ignored during the admissions review.

colleges.name: Revelle College
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking a highly structured, rigorous classical liberal arts and sciences foundation.
colleges.housing_profile: Traditional residential halls located on the southwest side of campus.

colleges.name: John Muir College
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Perfect for students desiring maximum flexibility in their general education requirements.
colleges.housing_profile: Located near the center of campus, offering a mix of residence halls and apartments.

colleges.name: Thurgood Marshall College
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Best for students passionate about social justice, public service, and diversity.
colleges.housing_profile: Ocean-view residential facilities located on the west side of campus.

colleges.name: Earl Warren College
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Great for engineering and STEM students, as it is located near the engineering buildings and encourages non-major exploration.
colleges.housing_profile: Situated near Geisel Library and the Jacobs School of Engineering.

colleges.name: Eleanor Roosevelt College
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students interested in global studies, international relations, and foreign languages.
colleges.housing_profile: Located in the northwest corner with a strong international community feel.

colleges.name: Sixth College
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Appeals to students interested in the intersection of culture, art, and modern technology.
colleges.housing_profile: Housed in the newer North Torrey Pines Living and Learning Neighborhood.

colleges.name: Seventh College
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students focused on interdisciplinary approaches to global environmental and societal shifts.
colleges.housing_profile: Transfer and upper-division focused, located near the Village.

colleges.name: Eighth College
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Designed for students committed to anti-racism, community engagement, and social justice.
colleges.housing_profile: Located in the state-of-the-art Theatre District Living and Learning Neighborhood.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Internal transfers are governed by a Selective Major points system prioritizing GPA, California residency, Pell eligibility, and first-generation status.
majors.notes: Direct admission from high school is fiercely competitive. Internal transfer is extremely difficult and relies on point-based lotteries after completing screening courses.

majors.name: Cognitive Science
majors.notes: Highly regarded internationally; integrates closely with the UCSD Design Lab.

majors.name: Biology
majors.notes: As of Spring 2025, Biological Sciences majors are no longer classified as selective for internal continuing students.

majors.name: Data Science
majors.special_requirements: Requires screening courses and competitive internal transfer application if not admitted directly.

application_deadlines.notes: The UC application submission window opens October 1.

supplemental_requirements.details: Applicants must choose 4 out of 8 Personal Insight Questions (PIQs), max 350 words each.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Internal transfer to Selective Majors utilizes a point system that awards extra points for Pell eligibility, California residency, and first-generation college status.

major_selection_tactics: Always select a non-capped major as your alternate choice to avoid outright rejection if denied from a selective program.
Avoid listing two capped majors on the application; UCSD will only evaluate the alternate if it is uncapped.
Apply directly to selective engineering or computer science fields as a high school senior, as internal transfers are incredibly restricted.
college_ranking_tactics: Rank the 8 residential colleges based on how well their General Education (GE) requirements overlap with your intended major's prerequisites.
Consider the college's philosophy and location on campus (e.g., Warren for Engineering proximity, Muir for flexibility).
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: UCSD admits students to the university first, then places them into majors. If a student applies to a capped major and isn't selected, they are guaranteed placement into their alternate major provided it is uncapped.

scholarships.name: Regents Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Priority enrollment, guaranteed housing, faculty mentorship, and research program placement.

scholarships.name: Undergraduate Research Scholarship (URS)
scholarships.benefits: Supports undergraduate students leading independent research projects.

ap_policy.general_rule: Grants elective or GE credit for AP scores of 3, 4, or 5.
exceptions: Maximum of 8 units awarded for passing both AP English Language and AP English Literature.
ap_policy.usage: Used primarily to fulfill minor/major prerequisites, elective units, or college-specific GE requirements, rather than full course exemptions in advanced STEM sequences.

ib_policy.general_rule: Grants 8 quarter units for Higher Level (HL) exams with a score of 5, 6, or 7. Standard Level (SL) exams yield no unit credit.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Pass/No Pass courses do not fulfill selective major transfer requirements. Courses must be completed with a C- or better.

what_it_takes: Rigorous A-G course load with maximum AP/IB/Honors classes.
A weighted UC GPA frequently exceeding 4.20.
Deep, sustained engagement in impactful extracurricular activities rather than superficial participation.
common_activities: Social justice advocacy and community service initiatives.
essay_tips: Use the PIQs to demonstrate resilience and personal growth.
Clearly articulate how your background aligns with your chosen major.
Focus on specific, quantifiable impacts made in leadership roles.
red_flags: Applying to two capped majors without selecting an uncapped alternate.
Lack of authentic voice or failing to directly answer the PIQ prompts.
insights: Your college ranking significantly alters your academic path; research their distinct General Education requirements deeply.
The 'UC Socially Dead' stereotype is avoidable if you actively join clubs like Tritons Roll Out or student co-ops.
The campus is huge; walking from residential colleges to central hubs like Geisel Library can take up to 20 minutes.
